  2. Intermediate Usage
   a.   File operations
 File Operations

                  Modes for Opening Files

                   ●   'r' : Read (default mode)
                   ●   'w' : Write (creates a new file or truncates an
                       existing file)
                   ●   'a' : Append (adds to the end of the file)
                   ●   'b' : Binary mode (used with other modes, e.g.,
                       'rb', 'wb')
                   ●   'x' : Create (creates a new file, fails if the file
                       already exists)
                   ●   't' : Text mode (default mode, can be used with
                       other modes, e.g., 'rt', 'wt')

 Contents
  3. Best Practice
   a.   Coding Style : PEP 8
   b.   Version Control : Anaconda
 PEP 8 - Code Layout
 ●   Indentation: Use 4 spaces per indentation level. Do not use tabs.

 ●   Maximum Line Length: Limit all lines to a maximum of 79 characters. For docstrings or comments, limit to 72
     characters.
 ●   Blank Lines: Separate top-level function and class definitions with two blank lines. Use a single blank line to
     separate methods within a class.
 PEP 8 - Imports
 ●   Import Modules: Imports should usually be on separate lines.

 ●   Import Order: Group imports in the following order:
       ○    Standard library imports.
       ○    Related third-party imports.
       ○    Local application/library-specific imports. Separate these groups with a blank line.
 ●   Avoid Wildcard Imports: Do not use wildcard imports (from module import *).
 PEP 8 - Whitespace
 ●   Avoid extraneous whitespace in the following situations:




 ●   No spaces before and after parentheses, brackets, or braces.




 ●   Use spaces around operators and after commas, colons, and semicolons:
 PEP 8 - Comments
 ●   Block Comments: Use block comments to explain code. Each line of a block comment starts with a # and a single
     space




 ●   Inline Comments: Use inline comments sparingly. Separate inline comments by at least two spaces from the
     statement.




 ●   Documentation Strings (Docstrings): Use triple quotes for docstrings, even for one-liners.
 PEP 8 - Naming Conventions
 ●   Function and Variable Names: Use lowercase words separated by underscores.




 ●   Class Names: Use the CapWords convention.




 ●   Constants: Use all uppercase letters with underscores separating words.




 ●   Method Names and Instance Variables: Use the lowercase_with_underscores convention.
 PEP 8 - Programming Recommendations
 ●   Avoid comparing directly to True, False, or None:




 ●   Use is for comparisons to None:




 ●   Use is not for comparisons to None:
 Contents
  3. Best Practice
   a.   Coding Style : PEP 8
   b.   Version Control : Anaconda
 Anaconda - https://www.anaconda.com/download
●   Conda allows you to create isolated environments for diﬀerent projects,
    ensuring that dependencies for one project don’t interfere with another.


●   Anaconda is a comprehensive distribution that includes Conda, Python, and
    over 1,500 scientiﬁc packages and their dependencies. It's designed to
    provide a ready-to-use environment for data science, machine learning, and
    scientiﬁc computing.


●   Miniconda: similar to Anaconda except Anaconda comes with a large number
    of pre-installed packages
 Anaconda - Windows Git Bash Conﬁguration
                                                       1.   ~/.bash_profile:



●   ~/.profile - runs for all login shell type

●   ~/.bash_profile - runs for login bash shell type
                                                        2. ~/.profile
●   ~/bashrc - run for non-login bash shell                Nothing as I am putting everything in ~/.bashrc

                                                        3. ~/.bashrc
 Anaconda - Creating and Managing Environments
 ●   Create a new environment: conda create --name myenv

 ●   Create an environment with specific Python version: conda create --name myenv python=3.12

 ●   Activate an environment: conda activate myenv

 ●   Deactivate an environment: conda deactivate

 ●   List all environments: conda env list

 ●   Remove an environment: conda remove --name myenv --all
 Anaconda - Installing and Managing Packages
 ●   Install a package: conda install numpy

 ●   Install a specific version of a package: conda install numpy=1.18

 ●   Install multiple packages at once: conda install numpy scipy matplotlib

 ●   List installed packages in the current environment: conda list

 ●   Update a package: conda update numpy

 ●   Update Conda itself: conda update conda

 ●   Remove a package: conda remove numpy
 Anaconda - Sharing Environments
 ●   Export an environment: conda env export > environment.yml

 ●   Create an environment from an exported YAML file: conda env create -f environment.yml
 Anaconda - Channels
 ●   View Current Channels: conda config --get channels

 ●   Add a New Channel: conda config --add channels conda-forge

 ●   Removing Channels: conda config --remove channels conda-forge

 ●   Install a Package from the New Channel: conda install -c conda-forge pandas

 ●   Set Strict Channel Priority(defaults channel first):conda config --set channel_priority strict
